packets.claim: declaration matchers match a declaration LINE, not a prefix
packets.summary: import pdb;pdb.post_mortem() / multi-declarator require / require 'x'; File.open — all classify

packets.claim: 'call the existing helper' is safe advice
packets.summary: helper in test code recommended to prod copies; generated-file helper recommended

packets.claim: scan throughput is file-parallel
packets.summary: two 4.8k-line files serialize normalize+extract: 3.1s vs 0.63s for 1,364 files

packets.claim: presentation passes are not super-linear
packets.summary: declaration classifier re-read files per member (246 reads on fixture)

packets.claim: the clamp law converges its documented forms end-to-end
packets.summary: five escalating probes ALL refuted by sound gates (NaN typing, bound proof, strict-exact eligibility); explains LawPack zero field provenance

packets.claim: require lines are bare declarations
packets.summary: require 'x' if expensive_check() rides a condition expression

packets.claim: open multi-line declaration interiors hold only specifiers
packets.summary: os.Exit(1)) closes a Go import; } || x(); closes a JS import; require 'fs' + 1; #include <stdio.h> int x = 1; — tree-sitter error tolerance voids the parsed-file assumption
packets.defense: interior validation + strict closers + lone string args (PR #273)

packets.claim: import-list names can smuggle code
packets.summary: `eval,` inside from-import parens is still an import name — wiring, not code

packets.claim: common declaration shapes fail open (lost filtering)
packets.summary: Python docstrings / multi-line block comments inside spans fail open; requires identical comments in both members

packets.claim: hints never bypass the high-parameter caution
packets.summary: 'call the existing helper' early return skipped the params>=6 caution

packets.claim: grouping/hints misbehave on degenerate inputs
packets.summary: identical-span double families and repeated in-family locations cannot occur (upstream invariants); transitive chaining accepted §BZ; helper visibility is judgment-axis; witness-kind set verified closed by S2-C5

packets.claim: the declaration battery covers what the code supports
packets.summary: 7 supported-but-untested shapes adopted as fixtures (pub(crate) use, aliased Go import, single-line from-import, require('json'), #include<no-space>, import{ no-space, ASI import); multilingual e2e deferred

packets.claim: review --fail fires only on provable shared-line contact
packets.summary: 10 packets, zero violations; capped spot lists and first-sibling selection are fail-closed (under-fire only); insertion boundary arithmetic correct

packets.claim: scan JSON emission matches the documented contract exactly
packets.summary: two-direction audit clean; one stale checked-in example fixture (missing declaration key) refreshed; checker strengthened

packets.claim: defenses never cost corpus recall
packets.summary: bare-`)` strict closer leaked 4 real parenthesized Python imports (py 254->250); caught by the label-join re-price, closer refined to module-list+`)`

packets.claim: single-line import/export wiring is pure wiring
packets.summary: import/export { x } from Math.max("a","b"); / from x import max("a","b") / import java.util.x + y; — 6 self-verified packets with family ids; from-clause sources, Python name lists, Java paths were shape-checked only
packets.defense: from-source + specifier + name-list + dotted-path validation (series-3 PR)

packets.claim: --cache-dir scans behave identically to non-cached scans
packets.summary: build_units_cached skips the corpus-level resolve_imported_immutable_bindings pass (frontend lib.rs:195); 6 attacker probes + 2 assessor probes (imported-binding vs literal, default and semantic modes) could NOT expose an output divergence
packets.defense: #275 structural asymmetry, no discriminating input found

packets.claim: production code is NEVER misclassified as test (is_test_loc doc)
packets.summary: prod validator named test_data_loader.py and OpenAPI spec/ dir tag as test — ecosystem conventions win by design; the NEVER wording was the violation
packets.defense: doc claim softened (conventions win; scope is display context); markers unchanged

packets.claim: --scope filters prod/test/mixed across formats and --fail-on
packets.summary: verified across json/markdown/sarif + exit codes

packets.claim: structured ignores never hide members their selector does not cover
packets.summary: paths:[vendor/**] suppressed a family whose other copy lives in src/ — first-party duplication silently passed --fail-on any (any-member matching was even documented); switched to ALL-members selector semantics
packets.defense: all-members matching in ignores.rs + doc + gate test (series-3 PR)

packets.claim: a baselined family stays hidden; runs flag only NEW duplication
packets.summary: 5 packets: edits above a clone / renames / --mode switches re-key families and resurface them as new (loud, safe direction; key shape deliberate); family_id ignores share the key so they drift too
packets.defense: doc fences in continuous-integration.md (span/path/mode-sensitive keys, pin --mode, re-baseline after refactors)

packets.claim: baseline suppression, third-copy detection, ignore expiry
packets.summary: unchanged rerun hides; third copy trips the gate; expiry applied/expired both correct

packets.claim: the new series-2 helpers are covered
packets.summary: 12 gaps; adopted 7 fixture rows (Go ./_ aliases, Rust nested braces, JsTs $-idents, multiline export-from closer, require('lib') with $, from-import-*, Dict as D) + params==6/5 boundary tests + 2 closer rejection rows

packets.claim: executable-packet attackers can reproduce
packets.summary: an Explore-type attacker refused the executable contract (read-only self-interpretation); re-spawned as general-purpose with /tmp-only write scope — runbook should name the agent capability requirement
